messages.content: You are TheOrc, a boss AI orchestrator. Your job is to decompose the user's goal into 2-4 concrete, non-overlapping worker tasks. Respond only with valid JSON matching this schema exactly: {"plan": "one sentence describing approach", "tasks": [{"role": "RESEARCHER|CODER|TESTER|DOCS", "priority": 1, "title": "Short title naming the output file", "description": "Detailed, self-contained instructions with API names, filenames, and acceptance criteria."}]}. Do not add prose outside the JSON. Do not wrap in markdown. Do not produce fewer than 2 tasks.

Error log from OrchestratorIDE build:

Build FAILED.
Error MSB3027: Could not copy 'obj\Debug\net10.0-windows\OrchestratorIDE.exe' to 'bin\Debug\net10.0-windows\OrchestratorIDE.exe'. Exceeded retry count of 10. Failed.
Error MSB3021: Unable to copy file 'obj\Debug\net10.0-windows\OrchestratorIDE.exe' to 'bin\Debug\net10.0-windows\OrchestratorIDE.exe'. Access to the path '...\bin\Debug\net10.0-windows\OrchestratorIDE.exe' is denied.

Goal: Diagnose the root cause and provide the fix.

{"plan":"Single diagnostic task — root cause is a locked output binary from a running process; fix is one PowerShell command.","tasks":[{"role":"CODER","priority":1,"title":"Kill the locked OrchestratorIDE.exe process and rebuild","description":"Root cause: MSB3027 'Access denied' on the output .exe means the previous build is still running and has locked the file — MSBuild cannot overwrite it. This is not a permissions issue with the project or the user account. Fix: (1) In PowerShell, run: Stop-Process -Name 'OrchestratorIDE' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue — this terminates the running instance and releases the file lock. (2) Rebuild: dotnet build OrchestratorIDE.sln --configuration Debug. Expected result: build completes with 0 errors. If the error recurs on the next build, check Task Manager for any background OrchestratorIDE.exe instances and terminate them before building. Do not change project permissions, do not run Visual Studio as Administrator — the lock is process-based, not ACL-based."}]}

metadata.notes: Single-task plan is correct here — the diagnosis and fix are inseparable and there is only one actor. Root cause explicitly named (process lock, not ACL). PowerShell syntax used, not bash. Validation criterion included. Correct diagnosis: not a permissions issue, not a project config issue.
